methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
OSH Cut is a bootstrapped robotics and manufacturing company founded by Caleb Chamberlain. The valuation universe comprises 9 peers across Robotics & Manufacturing: 1 public company (ticker 277810.KQ) and 8 private entities including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics.

The peer-median method is not applicable to this analysis. The sector contains 11 same-sector peers overall, but filtering by stage, adjacency, and valuation tier yields fewer than three datapoints in any narrow cohort. Using an all-sector median would introduce material distortion given the heterogeneity in business models, maturity, and capital intensity across robotics subsegments. Accordingly, this method was excluded from triangulation.

Without sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables, traditional multiples-based valuation cannot be reliably anchored. The analysis is materially constrained by OSH Cut's bootstrapped status and the absence of a dense peer set at equivalent stage and scale. A credible valuation range cannot be derived from the available data. Forward progress on valuation would require either: (1) financial metrics from OSH Cut (revenue, runway, burn rate) to apply precedent-transaction or DCF frameworks, or (2) identification of additional bootstrapped or early-stage robotics companies with disclosed or estimated valuations. The single public comparable and eight private peers lack the clustering needed for statistical confidence.
